# atender-application-engine

> An agent-orchestrated engine that produced a job application: the AI fleet, the video, and the
> portfolio. Built by Juan Camilo Posada (Choko) for the Atender Software Engineer role. The
> application IS the demonstration: an engineer who treats AI as substrate and automates his own work.

## What this repo is
- A sanitized orchestrator-worker agent fleet: one orchestrator + eight specialists + two review
  subagents, with a routing contract tested in CI.
- A Remotion video pipeline (video rendered in code) for the "normal Tuesday" cut.
- The voice pipeline and the OpenSpec change that planned the build.

## Proof
- `node .claude/scripts/validate-agent-fleet.mjs` validates routing + skill resolution + a
  secret-leak scan. It runs in CI on every push; green CI means the fleet contract holds.

## Author
Juan Camilo Posada (Choko). AI-native engineer. https://sockshead.github.io/portfolio/
GitHub https://github.com/Sockshead - LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/in/juan-camilo-posada/

## Honesty
Sanitized extraction of a fleet run on a private codebase. Product names, metrics, credentials,
and customer data are genericized or removed; the architecture, routing, gates, and CI are real.

## Video (the 60-second "normal Tuesday")
- Watch inline: https://sockshead.github.io/portfolio/watch.html
- File: media/atender-tuesday-60s.mp4 (60s) and media/atender-tuesday-15s.mp4 (15s)
- Captions: media/atender-tuesday-60s.vtt
- Machine-readable transcript + beat-by-beat description: media/VIDEO.md
- The video shows a real agent fleet validated in CI producing real green-CI work; rendered in code.
